title: Baghmara Pitcher Plant Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Baghmara Pitcher Plant Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Meghalaya

heroImage.alt: Baghmara Pitcher Plant Wildlife Sanctuary, Meghalaya

overview.short: Baghmara Pitcher plant Wildlife Sanctuary or Baghmara Wildlife Sanctuary or Baghmara Reserve Forest is a wildlife sanctuary located in the Indian state of Meghalaya.
overview.description: Baghmara Pitcher plant Wildlife Sanctuary or Baghmara Wildlife Sanctuary or Baghmara Reserve Forest is a wildlife sanctuary located in the Indian state of Meghalaya. This wildlife sanctuary is a protected area for pitcher plants. The site located near the city of Baghmara in South Garo Hills district. The total reserved forest occupies an area of 2.7 hectares (6.7 acres).

topPlaces.name: Malikona
topPlaces.description: Malikona is a village in Rongara sub-district, South Garo Hills district, Meghalaya, India. As of the 2011 census, it had 242 people.

topPlaces.name: Baghmara, Meghalaya
topPlaces.description: Baghmara (IPA: ˌbægˈmɑ:rə) is the headquarters of the South Garo Hills district in the state of Meghalaya in India. The place is bordered by the Mymensingh Division of Bangladesh and is about 113 km from Tura, 248 km from Guwahati, and 287 km from state capital Shillong.

topPlaces.name: Durgapur Upazila, Netrokona
topPlaces.description: Durgapur (Bengali: দূর্গাপুর) (also referred to as Susang Durgapur) is an upazila of the Netrokona District in the Mymensingh Division of Bangladesh.

items.note: Malikona is a village in Rongara sub-district, South Garo Hills district, Meghalaya, India. As of the 2011 census, it had 242 people.

Baghmara (IPA: ˌbægˈmɑ:rə) is the headquarters of the South Garo Hills district in the state of Meghalaya in India. The place is bordered by the Mymensingh Division of Bangladesh and is about 113 km from Tura, 248 km from Guwahati, and 287 km from state capital Shillong.
Durgapur (Bengali: দূর্গাপুর) (also referred to as Susang Durgapur) is an upazila of the Netrokona District in the Mymensingh Division of Bangladesh.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Baghmara Pitcher Plant Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Baghmara Pitcher Plant Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Baghmara Pitcher Plant Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, Guwahati (139 km) and the nearest railway station is Guwahati Railway Station (156 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Baghmara Pitcher Plant Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Baghmara Pitcher Plant Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Baghmara Pitcher Plant Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Baghmara Pitcher Plant Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Baghmara Pitcher Plant Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Malikona, Baghmara, Meghalaya, Durgapur Upazila, Netrokona.
